generatedSentence: The old builder, known for his naivety, would often looky at the ancient machinery, a vestige from a century when electric ripples powered everything. He'd flip through old relics, picturing a future where kangaroos wore pajamas, and even a prickly amberjack could absorb a waffle. His camera captured it all, a native son, feeling an emotional backward link to passwords and an unfaithful uniform, a true breaker of the mundane. One weekday, a new workload came, disrupting the known order: a colossal ruckus caused by a graffiti vandal, leaving a rotten stench and oddly placed keyhole carvings on skyscrapers. The builder, spotting beetles near an eject button, felt an outrage so strong it made him whack the nearest garbage jar, utterly flabbergasted. He knew this wasn't just goofy bafflegab but a contentious challenge, a link to something obscure buried deep within the urban gully, requiring a new approach to develop. The trail led to a nightclub where a jester, identical to the description, flicked a hanky at the door, initiating a bizarre concert. The officer, nominated for his noteworthy investigative skills, had to pass through a layover, picturing westerns where arbitration solved everything, yet this felt like the brink of a new kind of content. He spotted ectoplasm on a blackboard, a highlight that caused a full bodily uptake, then had to break a door to reveal a platformers' den filled with fluffy donut crumbs, all while his husband waited. Wearing a duster over his gabardine garment, the builder, now a beatific million-dollar man with a jetpack, flew over the glacier. He used a sacred teabag as a marker, knowing he had to develop a strategy against the villain, whose refusal to turn from his ways was frankly a kidney-punch to progress. He wouldn't knock on opportunity's door; he’d kick it open, ensuring the city's taintless future and honoring Newton's laws of order in the market.

rawPanelSentences: The old builder, known for his naivety, would often looky at the ancient machinery, a vestige from a century when electric ripples powered everything. He'd flip through old relics, picturing a future where kangaroos wore pajamas, and even a prickly amberjack could absorb a waffle. His camera captured it all, a native son, feeling an emotional backward link to passwords and an unfaithful uniform, a true breaker of the mundane.

One weekday, a new workload came, disrupting the known order: a colossal ruckus caused by a graffiti vandal, leaving a rotten stench and oddly placed keyhole carvings on skyscrapers. The builder, spotting beetles near an eject button, felt an outrage so strong it made him whack the nearest garbage jar, utterly flabbergasted. He knew this wasn't just goofy bafflegab but a contentious challenge, a link to something obscure buried deep within the urban gully, requiring a new approach to develop.

The trail led to a nightclub where a jester, identical to the description, flicked a hanky at the door, initiating a bizarre concert. The officer, nominated for his noteworthy investigative skills, had to pass through a layover, picturing westerns where arbitration solved everything, yet this felt like the brink of a new kind of content. He spotted ectoplasm on a blackboard, a highlight that caused a full bodily uptake, then had to break a door to reveal a platformers' den filled with fluffy donut crumbs, all while his husband waited.

Wearing a duster over his gabardine garment, the builder, now a beatific million-dollar man with a jetpack, flew over the glacier. He used a sacred teabag as a marker, knowing he had to develop a strategy against the villain, whose refusal to turn from his ways was frankly a kidney-punch to progress. He wouldn't knock on opportunity's door; he’d kick it open, ensuring the city's taintless future and honoring Newton's laws of order in the market.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined, grizzled builder wearing a high-tech exosuit and a luminous hardhat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The builder, in a dimly lit, futuristic workshop filled with holographic blueprints and antique tools, gazes thoughtfully at an old, glowing computer screen, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The builder, now in his exosuit, confronts a chaotic scene of neon-graffitied skyscrapers and robotic beetles swarming a keyhole-shaped entrance, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The builder, still in exosuit, cautiously enters a bizarre, glowing nightclub with strange jester-like figures dancing under pulsating lights, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The builder soars triumphantly above a futuristic cityscape, propelled by a sleek jetpack, his exosuit gleaming, looking towards a vast, illuminated glacier in the distance, dramatic high angle.

definitions.definition: Something that breaks.
A machine for breaking rocks, or for breaking coal at the mines
The building in which such a machine is placed.
A person who specializes in breaking things.
(chiefly in the plural) A wave breaking into foam against the shore, or against a sandbank, or a rock or reef near the surface, considered a useful warning to ships of an underwater hazard
A breakdancer.
A user of CB radio.
One who works in shipbreaking.
Oriented toward the back.
definitions.example: The battleship had three backwards guns at the stern, in addition to the primary complement.

definitions.definition: Reversed.
definitions.example: The backwards lettering on emergency vehicles makes it possible to read in the rear-view mirror.

definitions.definition: Behind current trends or technology.
definitions.example: Modern medicine regards the use of leeches as a backwards practice.

definitions.definition: Clumsy, inept, or inefficient, especially in learning.
definitions.example: He was a very backwards scholar, but he was a marvel on the football field.

definitions.definition: A person who builds or constructs things.
Master artisan, who receives his instructions from the architect, and employs workers.
A bodybuilder.
The mark of the foot left on the earth.
(by extension) A faint mark or visible sign left by something which is lost, or has perished, or is no longer present.
definitions.example: the vestiges of ancient magnificence in Palmyra

definitions.definition: A vestigial organ; a non-functional organ or body part that was once functional in an evolutionary ancestor.
The machines constituting a production apparatus, in a plant etc., collectively.
The working parts of a machine as a group.
The collective parts of something which allow it to function.
The literary devices used in a work, notably for dramatic effect
To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of or that.
definitions.example: He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.definition: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted or familiar with; to have encountered.
definitions.example: I know your mother, but I’ve never met your father.

definitions.definition: To experience.
definitions.example: Their relationship knew ups and downs.

definitions.definition: To be able to distinguish, to discern, particularly by contrast or comparison; to recognize the nature of.
definitions.example: I wouldn't know one from the other.

definitions.definition: To recognize as the same (as someone or something previously encountered) after an absence or change.
To understand or have a grasp of through experience or study.
definitions.example: His mother tongue is Italian, but he also knows French and English.

definitions.definition: To have sexual relations with. This meaning normally specified in modern English as e.g. to ’know someone in the biblical sense’ or to ‘know Biblically.’
To have knowledge; to have information, be informed.
definitions.example: He knows about 19th century politics.

definitions.definition: To be or become aware or cognizant.
definitions.example: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.definition: To be acquainted (with another person).
To be able to play or perform (a song or other piece of music).
definitions.example: Do you know "Blueberry Hill"?

definitions.definition: Any fact or situation which is known or familiar.
A constant or variable the value of which is already determined.
Identified as a specific type; famous, renowned.
definitions.example: He was a known pickpocket.

definitions.definition: Accepted, familiar, researched.
A member of the Macropodidae family of large marsupials with strong hind legs for hopping, native to Australia.
A hooded jacket with a front pocket, usually of fleece material, a kangaroo jacket.
To practice kangaroo care on an infant; to hold a premature infant against the skin.
To hunt kangaroo.
To move like a kangaroo
Of or relating to Australia.
(investments) Characterized by rapidly jumping prices in securities markets or by belief that the prices are unstable in contrast to bear and bull markets.
Lack of sophistication, experience, judgement or worldliness; artlessness; gullibility; credulity.
An aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
Aboriginal inhabitant of a region colonized by English-speaking people; in particular:
A person who is native to a place; a person who was born in a place.
A native speaker.
Ostrea edulis, a kind of oyster.
Belonging to one by birth.
definitions.example: English is not my native language.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of or relating to people inhabiting a region from prehistoric times.
definitions.example: The native peoples of Australia are called aborigines.

definitions.definition: Born or grown in the region in which it lives or is found; not foreign or imported.
definitions.example: Many native artists studied abroad.

definitions.definition: (of a species) Which occurs of its own accord in a given locality, to be contrasted with a species introduced by man.
definitions.example: The naturalized Norway maple often outcompetes the native North American sugar maple.

definitions.definition: (of software) Pertaining to the system or architecture in question.
definitions.example: The native integer size is sixteen bits.

definitions.definition: Occurring naturally in its pure or uncombined form; native aluminium, native salt.
Arising by birth; having an origin; born.
Original; constituting the original substance of anything.
definitions.example: native dust

definitions.definition: Naturally related; cognate; connected (with).
A word used to gain admittance or to gain access to information; watchword.
A string of characters used to log in to a computer or network, to access a level in a video game, etc.
To protect with a password.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
Something that gives a pricking sensation; a sharp object.
Covered with sharp points.
definitions.example: The prickly pear is a cactus; you have to peel it before eating it to remove the spines and the tough skin.

definitions.definition: Easily irritated.
definitions.example: He has a prickly personality. He doesn't get along with people because he is easily set off.

definitions.definition: Difficult; complicated; (figuratively) hairy or thorny.
definitions.example: It was a prickly situation.

definitions.definition: In a prickly manner.

A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.

A unit in ancient Roman army, originally of 100 army soldiers as part of a cohort, later of more varied sizes (but typically containing 60 to 70 or 80) soldiers or other men (guards, police, firemen), commanded by a centurion.
A political division of ancient Rome, meeting in the Centuriate Assembly.
A hundred things of the same kind; a hundred.
A hundred runs scored either by a single player in one innings, or by two players in a partnership.
A score of one hundred points.
definitions.example: That was his tenth professional century.

definitions.definition: A race a hundred units (as meters, kilometres, miles) in length.
A banknote in the denomination of one hundred dollars.
A device for taking still or moving pictures or photographs.
The viewpoint in a three-dimensional game or simulation.
A vaulted room.
The judge's private chamber, where cases may be heard in camera.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The brunch was waffles with strawberries and whipped cream.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
That which remains; that which is left after loss or decay; a remaining portion.
Something old and outdated, possibly kept for sentimental reasons.
A part of the body of a saint, or an ancient religious object, kept for veneration.
(often of guitars) To cause (an object) to appear old or worn, to distress.
A moving disturbance, or undulation, in the surface of a fluid.
definitions.example: I dropped a small stone into the pond and watched the ripples spread.

definitions.definition: A sound similar to that of undulating water.
A style of ice cream in which flavors have been coarsely blended together.
definitions.example: I enjoy fudge ripple ice cream, but I especially like to dig through the carton to get at the ripple part and eat only that.

definitions.definition: A small oscillation of an otherwise steady signal.
To move like the undulating surface of a body of water; to undulate.
To propagate like a moving wave.
To make a sound as of water running gently over a rough bottom, or the breaking of ripples on the shore.
To shape into a series of ripples.
To launch or unleash in rapid succession.
A maneuver which rotates an object end over end.
definitions.example: The diver did a couple of flips before landing in the pool.

definitions.definition: A complete change of direction, decision, movement etc.
A slingshot.
A hairstyle popular among boys in the 1960s–70s and 2000s–10s, in which the hair goes halfway down the ears, at which point it sticks out
definitions.example: Justin Bieber and Zac Efron are among the celebrities who wore a flip.

definitions.definition: The purchase of an asset (usually a house) which is then improved and sold quickly for profit.
To throw so as to turn over.
definitions.example: You need to flip the pancake onto the other side.

definitions.definition: To put into a quick revolving motion through a snap of the thumb and index finger.
definitions.example: If you can't decide which option to go for, flip a coin.

definitions.definition: To win a state (or county) won by another party in the preceding elections
definitions.example: Wisconsin had been Democratic for decades, but the Republicans flipped it in 2016.

definitions.definition: To turn state's evidence; to agree to testify against one's co-conspirators in exchange for concessions from prosecutors.
definitions.example: The mafioso flipped on his superiors to get a lighter sentence.

definitions.definition: To induce someone to turn state's evidence; to get someone to agree to testify against their co-conspirators in exchange for concessions.
definitions.example: The district attorney was able to strengthen his case against the bank robber by flipping the getaway driver.

definitions.definition: To go berserk or crazy.
definitions.example: I'd flip if anyone broke my phone.

definitions.definition: To buy an asset (usually a house), improve it and sell it quickly for profit.
To invert a bit (binary digit), changing it from 0 to 1 or from 1 to 0.
Of or relating to the emotions.
definitions.example: emotional crisis

definitions.definition: Characterised by emotion.
Determined by emotion rather than reason.
definitions.example: emotional decision

definitions.definition: Appealing to or arousing emotion.
definitions.example: emotional speech

definitions.definition: Easily affected by emotion.
definitions.example: She’s an emotional person.

definitions.definition: Readily displaying emotion.
definitions.example: emotional greeting

definitions.definition: (usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
(sometimes humorous) Look.
To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up.
To engulf, as in water; to swallow up.
To suck up; to drink in; to imbibe, like a sponge or as the lacteals of the body; to chemically take in.
To take in energy and convert it, as
definitions.example: Heat, light, and electricity are absorbed in the substances into which they pass.

definitions.definition: To engross or engage wholly; to occupy fully
To occupy or consume time.
Assimilate mentally.
To assume or pay for as part of a commercial transaction.
To defray the costs.
To accept or purchase in quantity.
Any of several large food and game yellowtail fishes of the genus Seriola, found in warm waters of all oceans.
Not having religious faith.
Not keeping good faith; disloyal; not faithful.
Adulterous.
Not honest or upright.
Negligent or imperfect.
definitions.example: The painting was an unfaithful rendering of its subject.

definitions.definition: Not faithfully rendering the meaning of the source language; incorrect.
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
A small car, the Volkswagen Beetle (original version made 1938–2003, similar models made 1997–2010 and since 2011)
Any of numerous species of insect in the order Coleoptera characterized by a pair of hard, shell-like front wings which cover and protect a pair of rear wings when at rest.
A game of chance in which players attempt to complete a drawing of a beetle, different dice rolls allowing them to add the various body parts.
To move away quickly, to scurry away.
definitions.example: He beetled off on his vacation.

definitions.definition: To loom over; to extend or jut.
definitions.example: The heavy chimney beetled over the thatched roof.

definitions.definition: A type of mallet with a large wooden head, used to drive wedges, beat pavements, etc.
A machine in which fabrics are subjected to a hammering process while passing over rollers, as in cotton mills; a beetling machine.
To beat with a heavy mallet.
To finish by subjecting to a hammering process in a beetle or beetling machine.
definitions.example: to beetle cotton goods

definitions.definition: A person who needlessly destroys, defaces, or damages other people's property.
A trench, ravine or narrow channel which was worn by water flow, especially on a hillside.
A small valley.
A drop kerb.
A road drain.
A fielding position on the off side about 30 degrees behind square, between the slips and point; a fielder in such a position
A grooved iron rail or tram plate.
To flow noisily.
To wear away into a gully or gullies.
The hole in a lock where the key is inserted and turns.
Any small opening resembling the hole for a key in shape or function.
A circle cut out of a garment as a decorative effect, typically at the front or back neckline of a dress.
A hole or excavation in beams intended to be joined together, to receive the key that fastens them.
A mortise for a key or cotter.
(lasers) A transient column of vapor or plasma formed when using high energy beams, such as lasers, for welding or cutting.
definitions.example: keyhole plasma

definitions.definition: A welding method in which a hole forms in the surface immediately ahead of the puddle in the direction of welding. The hole is filled as the weld progresses.
The free-throw lane together with the circle surrounding the free-throw line; key.
A gravitational keyhole.

(ordnance) To strike a target after wobbling in flight so that the long axis of the bullet does not follow the line of flight; typically due to insufficient spin resulting from the rifling in the barrel.
A go-cart.
To ride in a go-cart.

A traditional Scottish garment, usually worn by men, having roughly the same morphology as a wrap-around skirt, with overlapping front aprons and pleated around the sides and back, and usually made of twill-woven worsted wool with a tartan pattern.
Any Scottish garment from which the above lies in a direct line of descent, such as the philibeg, or the great kilt or belted plaid
A plaid, pleated school uniform skirt sometimes structured as a wrap around, sometimes pleated throughout the entire circumference; also used as boys' wear in 19th century USA.
A variety of non-bifurcated garments made for men and loosely resembling a Scottish kilt, but most often made from different fabrics and not always with tartan plaid designs.
To gather up (skirts) around the body.
An earthenware container, either with two or no handles, for holding oil, water, wine, etc., or used for burial.
A small, approximately cylindrical container, normally made of clay or glass, for holding fruit, preserves, etc., or for ornamental purposes.
A jar and its contents; as much as fills such a container; a jarful.
To preserve (food) in a jar.
Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
An insect in the order Orthoptera, especially family Gryllidae, that makes a chirping sound by rubbing its wing casings against combs on its hind legs.
A wooden footstool.
A signalling device used by soldiers in hostile territory to identify themselves to a friendly in low visibility conditions.
A relatively small area of a roof constructed to divert water from a horizontal intersection of the roof with a chimney, wall, expansion joint or other projection.
Agreement in a design or plan; union formed by mutual communication of opinions and views; accordance in a scheme; harmony; simultaneous action.
Musical accordance or harmony; concord.
A musical entertainment in which several voices or instruments take part.
definitions.example: I'm going to the rock concert on Friday.

definitions.definition: To plan together; to settle or adjust by conference, agreement, or consultation.
To plan; to devise; to arrange.
To act in harmony or conjunction; to form combined plans.
Egregious.
Bad (not good), inauthentic, of an inferior quality, contemptible, lacking integrity, lame, or strange.
definitions.example: Every record they ever made was straight-up wack.

definitions.definition: Crazy, mad, insane.
Cool, bizarre, and potentially scary.
The sound of a heavy strike.
The strike itself.
The stroke itself, regardless of its successful impact.
An attempt, a chance, a turn, a go, originally an attempt to beat someone or something.
definitions.example: 40 bucks a whack.

definitions.definition: (originally Britain cant) A share, a portion, especially a full share or large portion.
A whack-up: a division of an amount into separate whacks, a divvying up.
A deal, an agreement.
definitions.example: It's a whack!

definitions.definition: The backslash, ⟨ \ ⟩.
To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Of perishable items, overridden with bacteria and other infectious agents.
definitions.example: If you leave a bin unattended for a few weeks, the rubbish inside will turn rotten.

definitions.definition: In a state of decay.
definitions.example: His mouth stank and his teeth were rotten.

definitions.definition: Cruel, mean or immoral.
definitions.example: That man is a rotten father.

definitions.definition: Bad or terrible.
definitions.example: It was a rotten idea to take the boat out today.

definitions.definition: To an extreme degree.
definitions.example: That kid is spoilt rotten.

definitions.definition: A noisy disturbance and/or commotion.
A row, fight.
An excessively violent or vicious attack; an atrocity.
An offensive, immoral or indecent act.
The resentful anger aroused by such acts.
A destructive rampage.
To cause or commit an outrage upon; to treat with violence or abuse.
To violate; to rape (a female).
To rage in excess of.
Arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
A position in an arrangement, disposition, or sequence.
The state of being well arranged.
definitions.example: The house is in order; the machinery is out of order.

definitions.definition: Conformity with law or decorum; freedom from disturbance; general tranquillity; public quiet.
definitions.example: to preserve order in a community or an assembly

definitions.definition: A command.
A request for some product or service; a commission to purchase, sell, or supply goods.
A group of religious adherents, especially monks or nuns, set apart within their religion by adherence to a particular rule or set of principles.
definitions.example: St. Ignatius Loyola founded the Jesuit order in 1537.

definitions.definition: An association of knights.
definitions.example: the Order of the Garter, the Order of the Bath.

definitions.definition: Any group of people with common interests.
A decoration, awarded by a government, a dynastic house, or a religious body to an individual, usually for distinguished service to a nation or to humanity.
A rank in the classification of organisms, below class and above family; a taxon at that rank.
definitions.example: Magnolias belong to the order Magnoliales.

definitions.definition: A number of things or persons arranged in a fixed or suitable place, or relative position; a rank; a row; a grade; especially, a rank or class in society; a distinct character, kind, or sort.
definitions.example: talent of a high order

definitions.definition: (chiefly plural) An ecclesiastical grade or rank, as of deacon, priest, or bishop; the office of the Christian ministry.
definitions.example: to take orders, or to take holy orders, that is, to enter some grade of the ministry

definitions.definition: The disposition of a column and its component parts, and of the entablature resting upon it, in classical architecture; hence (since the column and entablature are the characteristic features of classical architecture) a style or manner of architectural design.
The sequence in which a side’s batsmen bat; the batting order.
A power of polynomial function in an electronic circuit’s block, such as a filter, an amplifier, etc.
definitions.example: a 3-stage cascade of a 2nd-order bandpass Butterworth filter

definitions.definition: The overall power of the rate law of a chemical reaction, expressed as a polynomial function of concentrations of reactants and products.
The cardinality, or number of elements in a set, group, or other structure regardable as a set.

(of an element of a group) For given group G and element g ∈ G, the smallest positive natural number n, if it exists, such that (using multiplicative notation), gn = e, where e is the identity element of G; if no such number exists, the element is said to be of infinite order (or sometimes zero order).
The number of vertices in a graph.
A partially ordered set.
The relation on a partially ordered set that determines that it is, in fact, a partially ordered set.
The sum of the exponents on the variables in a monomial, or the highest such among all monomials in a polynomial.
definitions.example: A quadratic polynomial, a x^2 + b x + c, is said to be of order (or degree) 2.

definitions.definition: A written direction to furnish someone with money or property; compare money order, postal order.
To set in some sort of order.
To arrange, set in proper order.
To issue a command to.
definitions.example: He ordered me to leave.

definitions.definition: To request some product or service; to secure by placing an order.
definitions.example: to order groceries

definitions.definition: To admit to holy orders; to ordain; to receive into the ranks of the ministry.
A very tall building with a large number of floors.
A small sail atop a mast of a ship; a triangular skysail.
Anything very tall or high.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
Completely, entirely, to the fullest extent

definitions.definition: A type of woolen cloth with a diagonal ribbed texture on one side.

definitions.definition: A similar fabric, made from cotton.
A gaberdine (garment).
A yellow robe that Jews in England were compelled to wear in the year 1189 as a mark of distinction.
(long and short scales) The cardinal number 1,000,000: 106; a thousand thousand.
An unspecified very large number.
definitions.example: I can think of millions of reasons not to go.

definitions.definition: A carnivorous aquatic mammal in one of several families of order Cetacea, famed for its intelligence and occasional willingness to approach humans.
A fish, the mahi-mahi or dorado, Coryphaena hippurus, with a dorsal fin that runs the length of the body, also known for iridescent coloration.
A depiction of a fish, with a broad indented fin, usually embowed.
The dauphin, eldest son of the kings of France.
(history) A mass of iron or lead hung from the yardarm, in readiness to be dropped through the deck and the hull of an enemy's vessel to sink it.
A kind of wreath or strap of plaited cordage.
A spar or buoy held by an anchor and furnished with a ring to which ships may fasten their cables.
A mooring post on a wharf or beach.
A permanent fender around a heavy boat just below the gunwale.
One of the handles above the trunnions by which a gun was lifted.
An organ in the body that filters the blood, producing urine.
This organ (of an animal) cooked as food.
Constitution, temperament, nature, type, character, disposition. (usually used of people)
A waiter.
(heading) to make a non-linear physical movement.
(heading) To change condition or attitude.
To change one's course of action; to take a new approach.
(usually with over) To complete.
definitions.example: They say they can turn the parts in two days.

definitions.definition: To make (money); turn a profit.
definitions.example: We turned a pretty penny with that little scheme.

definitions.definition: Of a player, to go past an opposition player with the ball in one's control.
To undergo the process of turning on a lathe.
definitions.example: Ivory turns well.

definitions.definition: To bring down the feet of a child in the womb, in order to facilitate delivery.
To invert a type of the same thickness, as a temporary substitute for any sort which is exhausted.
To translate.
definitions.example: to turn the Iliad

definitions.definition: To magically or divinely attack undead.
In the International System of Units, the derived unit of force; the force required to accelerate a mass of one kilogram by one metre per second per second. Symbol: N.
To represent in or with a picture.
To imagine or envision.
To depict or describe vividly.
Depiction

A film, or some other dramatic work, set in, the historic (c. 1850-1910) American West (west of the Mississippi river) focusing on conflict between whites and Indians, lawmen and outlaws, ranchers and farmers, or industry (railroads, mining) and agriculture.
The amount of work assigned to a particular worker, normally in a specified time period
The amount of work that a machine can handle or produce
An impassioned, disputatious public speech.
A tirade, harsh scolding or rant, whether spoken or written.
definitions.example: She gave her son a harangue about the dangers of playing in the street.

definitions.definition: To give a forceful and lengthy lecture or criticism to someone.
definitions.example: The angry motorist leapt from his car to harangue the other driver.

definitions.definition: The act of refusing.
Depth or point at which well or borehole drilling cannot continue.
Someone or something that has a fluffy texture.
A person who is superficial, who lacks depth or seriousness.
A babycino (frothy milk drink).
Covered with fluff.
Light; soft; airy.
Warm and comforting.
Not clearly defined or explained; fuzzy.
Lightweight; superficial; lacking depth or seriousness.
(manner) In a frank, open or (too) honest manner.
definitions.example: He spoke frankly about the economy.

definitions.definition: (sentence adverb) In truth, to tell the truth.
Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.
definitions.example: Click the hyperlink to go to the next page.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An address, URL, or program that defines a hyperlink's function.
definitions.example: Copy the hyperlink and paste it into an email.

definitions.definition: A connection between places, people, events, things, or ideas.
definitions.example: The mayor’s assistant serves as the link to the media.

definitions.definition: One element of a chain or other connected series.
definitions.example: The third link of the silver chain needs to be resoldered.

definitions.definition: The connection between buses or systems.
definitions.example: A by-N-link is composed of N lanes.

definitions.definition: A space comprising one or more disjoint knots.
A thin wild bank of land splitting two cultivated patches and often linking two hills.
An individual person or element in a system
Anything doubled and closed like a link of a chain.
definitions.example: a link of horsehair

definitions.definition: A sausage that is not a patty.

(kinematics) Any one of the several elementary pieces of a mechanism, such as the fixed frame, or a rod, wheel, mass of confined liquid, etc., by which relative motion of other parts is produced and constrained.

Any intermediate rod or piece for transmitting force or motion, especially a short connecting rod with a bearing at each end; specifically (in steam engines) the slotted bar, or connecting piece, to the opposite ends of which the eccentric rods are jointed, and by means of which the movement of the valve is varied, in a link motion.
The length of one joint of Gunter's chain, being the hundredth part of it, or 7.92 inches, the chain being 66 feet in length.
A bond of affinity, or a unit of valence between atoms; applied to a unit of chemical force or attraction.
(plural) The windings of a river; the land along a winding stream.
To connect two or more things.
(of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.
definitions.example: My homepage links to my wife's.

definitions.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.
definitions.example: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.

definitions.definition: To post a hyperlink to.
definitions.example: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

definitions.definition: To demonstrate a correlation between two things.
To combine objects generated by a compiler into a single executable.
Placed in a grave at a burial.
Concealed, hidden.
To ritualistically inter in a grave or tomb.
To place in the ground.
definitions.example: bury a bone;  bury the embers

definitions.definition: To hide or conceal as if by covering with earth or another substance.
definitions.example: she buried her face in the pillow;  they buried us in paperwork

definitions.definition: To suppress and hide away in one's mind.
definitions.example: secrets kept buried; she buried her shame and put on a smiling face.

definitions.definition: To put an end to; to abandon.
definitions.example: They buried their argument and shook hands.

definitions.definition: To score a goal.
To kill or murder.
To render imperceptible by other, more prominent stimuli; drown out.

definitions.definition: A large flat surface, finished with black slate or a similar material, that can be written upon with chalk and subsequently erased; a chalkboard.
To use a blackboard to assist in an informal discussion.
Pretentious, incomprehensible, or overly technical language, especially legal or bureaucratic jargon.
To use pretentious, incomprehensible, or overly technical language.
An instance of breaking something into two or more pieces.
definitions.example: The femur has a clean break and so should heal easily.

definitions.definition: A physical space that opens up in something or between two things.
definitions.example: He waited minutes for a break in the traffic to cross the highway.

definitions.definition: A rest or pause, usually from work.
definitions.example: Let’s take a five-minute break.

definitions.definition: A time for students to talk or play.
A short holiday.
definitions.example: a weekend break on the Isle of Wight

definitions.definition: A temporary split with a romantic partner.
definitions.example: I think we need a break.

definitions.definition: An interval or intermission between two parts of a performance, for example a theatre show, broadcast, or sports game.
A significant change in circumstance, attitude, perception, or focus of attention.

definitions.definition: The beginning (of the morning).
definitions.example: at the break of day

definitions.definition: An act of escaping.
definitions.example: It was a clean break.

definitions.definition: The separation between lines, paragraphs or pages of a written text.
A keystroke or other signal that causes a program to terminate or suspend execution.
A point or condition in a program at which operation may be suspended during debugging so that the state of the program at that point can be investigated. A breakpoint.
A change, particularly the end of a spell of persistent good or bad weather.
A large four-wheeled carriage, having a straight body and calash top, with the driver's seat in front and the footman's behind.
(equitation) A sharp bit or snaffle.
A short section of music, often between verses, in which some performers stop while others continue.

definitions.definition: The point in the musical scale at which a woodwind instrument is designed to overblow, that is, to move from its lower to its upper register.
definitions.example: Crossing the break smoothly is one of the first lessons the young clarinettist needs to master.

definitions.definition: Usu. plural An area along a river that features steep banks, bluffs, or gorges (e.g., Upper Missouri River Breaks National Monument, US).
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
A large body of ice which flows under its own mass, usually downhill.

definitions.definition: Silly, quirky
A gathering of people for the purchase and sale of merchandise at a set time, often periodic.

definitions.definition: City square or other fairly spacious site where traders set up stalls and buyers browse the merchandise.
A grocery store

definitions.definition: A geographical area where a certain commercial demand exists.

definitions.definition: The sum total traded in a process of individuals trading for certain commodities.
The price for which a thing is sold in a market; hence, value; worth.
To make (products or services) available for sale and promote them.

definitions.definition: To sell

definitions.definition: To deal in a market; to buy or sell; to make bargains for provisions or goods.
A deep-fried piece of dough or batter, commonly made in a toroidal or ellipsoidal shape, and mixed with various sweeteners and flavors, sometimes filled with jelly, custard or cream.
Anything in the shape of a torus.

A spare tire, smaller and less durable than a full-sized tire, only intended for temporary use.
A toroidal cushion typically used by hemorrhoid patients.
An idiot.

definitions.definition: A squalid or wretched place; a haunt.

definitions.definition: A comfortable room not used for formal entertaining.
A narrow glen; a ravine; a dell.
To ensconce or hide oneself in (or as in) a den.

A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. Doors are frequently made of wood or metal. May have a handle to help open and close, a latch to hold the door closed and a lock that ensures the door cannot be opened without the key.

definitions.definition: Any flap, etc. that opens like a door.

definitions.definition: (immigration) An entry point.
A means of approach or access.

definitions.definition: A barrier.

To cause a collision by opening the door of a vehicle in front of an oncoming cyclist or pedestrian.
One who jests, jokes or teases.
A person in colourful garb and fool's cap who amused a medieval and early modern royal or noble court.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the Southeast Asian genus Symbrenthia.
(usually pluralized) Something which has exactly the same properties as something else.
An identical twin.
Bearing full likeness by having precisely the same set of characteristics; indistinguishable.
Not different or other; not another or others; not different as regards self; selfsame; numerically identical.
Of twins, sharing the same genetic code.
Exactly equivalent.
Approximating or approaching exact equivalence.
To name someone as a candidate for a particular role or position, including that of an office.
To entitle, confer a name upon.

Deserving attention; notable; worthy of notice.

definitions.definition: A platform game.

Any day of the week (Monday through Sunday).

A tiller of the ground; a husbandman.
A prudent or frugal manager.
A man in a marriage or marital relationship, especially in relation to his spouse.

definitions.definition: The male of a pair of animals.
A manager of property; one who has the care of another's belongings, owndom, or interests; a steward; an economist.
A large cushion with arms meant to support a person in the sitting position.

definitions.definition: A polled tree; a pollard.
To manage or administer carefully and frugally; use to the best advantage; economise.

definitions.definition: To conserve.
To till; cultivate; farm; nurture.
To provide with a husband.
To engage or act as a husband to; assume the care of or responsibility for; accept as one's own.
A piece of cloth, usually square and often fine and elegant, carried for wiping the face, eyes, nose or hands.
A piece of cloth shaped like a handkerchief to be worn about the neck; a neckerchief or neckcloth.
To compel (a person or persons) to leave.

definitions.definition: To throw out or remove forcefully.

definitions.definition: To come out of a machine.

The outer granule-free layer of cytoplasm.
A break between stages of a journey.

definitions.definition: The act or process of arbitrating.
A process through which two or more parties use an arbitrator or arbiter in order to resolve a dispute.

In general, a form of justice where both parties designate a person whose ruling they will accept formally. More specifically in Market Anarchist (market anarchy) theory, arbitration designates the process by which two agencies pre-negotiate a set of common rules in anticipation of cases where a customer from each agency is involved in a dispute.
Understanding; comprehension.
Absorption, especially of food or nutrient by an organism.
The act of lifting or taking up.
A chimney.
The upcast pipe from the smokebox of a steam boiler towards the chimney.
To take up, to lift.
To absorb, as food or a drug by an organism.
To accept and begin to use, as a new practice.
Blessed, blissful, heavenly
Having a benign appearance
The edge, margin, or border of a steep place, as of a precipice; a bank or edge.
definitions.example: the brink of a river

definitions.definition: The edge or border
definitions.example: He's on the brink of madness.

definitions.definition: A single item of clothing.
Short for temple garment.
To clothe in a garment.
An object, now especially a cloth, used for dusting surfaces etc.
Someone who dusts.
A light, loose-fitting long coat.

(milling) A blowing-machine for separating the flour from the bran.
(oil and gas) A dry drill hole, one that does not produce oil or gas.
A vehicle-mounted, multi-barrelled, anti-aircraft gun.
A sundress.
A block of felt strips, shaped ergonomically, used to remove chalk from a blackboard.
A high pitch toward the batter.
To change with a specific direction, progress.

definitions.definition: To progress through a sequence of stages.

definitions.definition: To advance; to further; to promote the growth of.
To create.
definitions.example: I need to develop a plan for the next three weeks.

definitions.definition: To bring out images latent in photographic film.

definitions.definition: To acquire something usually over a period of time.

definitions.definition: To place one's pieces actively.
definitions.example: I need to develop my white-square bishop.

definitions.definition: To cause a ball to become more open and available to be played on later. Usually by moving it away from the cushion, or by opening a pack.
To change the form of (an algebraic expression, etc.) by executing certain indicated operations without changing the value.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.

definitions.definition: A sharp impact.

definitions.definition: Criticism.
Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.

definitions.definition: Spotless; totally clean; free from blemish
A cloth or paper sachet containing tea leaves or herbal tea, designed to act as an infuser when submerged in hot water.
To lower one’s testicles into the mouth of another person, or onto the face or head of another person.
To sit or crouch repeatedly on the head of another player's character after they are dead.
A piece of equipment that may be strapped to a person’s back and generate vertical thrust by a downward discharge of jet fluid, thereby rendering the person capable of flight.
To travel with a jet pack.

definitions.definition: A public or private establishment that is open late at night to provide entertainment, food, drink, music and/or dancing.
A strip club.
To visit a nightclub (or nightclubs) for entertainment.
definitions.example: Our first night in the big city we went out nightclubbing. The next morning we stayed in hung over.

definitions.definition: To change place.
To change in state or status
To move through time.
To be accepted.
In any game, to decline to play in one's turn.
To do or be better.
To take heed.
Marked by heated arguments or controversy.
Given to struggling with others out of jealousy or discord.
An area or a spot in a drawing, painting, or photograph that is strongly illuminated.
An especially significant or interesting detail or event or period of time.
definitions.example: Meeting my future wife was the highlight of my trip to Spain.

definitions.definition: A strand or spot of hair dyed a different color than the rest.
To make prominent; emphasize.
To be a highlight of.
To mark (important passages of text), e.g. with a fluorescent marker pen or in a wordprocessor, as a means of memory retention or for later reference.
To dye (part of the hair) a different color than the rest.
To seek the attention of (a user) on IRC by mentioning their name in a message, causing that message to appear highlighted on their screen.
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.

definitions.definition: To overwhelm with bewilderment; to amaze, confound, or stun, especially in a ludicrous manner.

definitions.definition: Appalled, annoyed, exhausted or disgusted.

definitions.definition: Damned.
One who has a position of authority in a hierarchical organization, especially in military, police or government organizations.
A respectful term of address for an officer, especially a police officer.
One who holds a public office.
An agent or servant imparted with the ability, to some degree, to act on initiative.
A commissioned officer.
To supply with officers.
To command like an officer.
In a peculiar manner; strangely; unusually.

definitions.definition: In a manner measured by an odd number.
To render obscure; to darken; to make dim; to keep in the dark; to hide; to make less visible, intelligible, legible, glorious, beautiful, or illustrious.
To hide, put out of sight etc.
To conceal oneself; to hide.
Dark, faint or indistinct.
Hidden, out of sight or inconspicuous.
Difficult to understand.

definitions.definition: Not well-known.
Unknown or uncertain; unclear.

definitions.definition: Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.

definitions.definition: Religious; relating to religion, or to the services of religion; not secular
Spiritual; concerned with metaphysics.
Designated or exalted by a divine sanction; possessing the highest title to obedience, honor, reverence, or veneration; entitled to extreme reverence; venerable.
Not to be profaned or violated; inviolable.
(followed by the preposition "to") Consecrated; dedicated; devoted
Solemnly devoted, in a bad sense, as to evil, vengeance, curse, or the like; accursed; baleful.
